content.text: Now let me look at the FBT controller — it has `eslint-disable` comments that are now "unused" because the code still has warnings on the same lines. Let me read the controller.

The issue is clear: there are `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` comments on lines 39, 69, 101, but the ESLint output says those disable directives are "unused" (no problems reported). Yet lines 41-42, 71-72, 103-104 still have warnings for `no-unnecessary-condition`. 

The `eslint-disable-next-line` is placed on the comment line (39), not on the actual offending line (41). The disable directive is on line 39 but the code with warnings is on lines 41-42. The formatter function spans multiple lines so the comment doesn't cover the right lines.

Looking at the ESLint output again:
- Line 39: "Unused eslint-disable directive" 
- Lines 41-42: still have `no-unnecessary-condition` warnings

The `eslint-disable-next-line` only suppresses warnings on the immediately next line. The `res?.frequently_bought_together?.length` is on line 41 (two lines after the comment). 

The correct fix is to use `eslint-disable` / `eslint-enable` block comments or move the disable comment to be directly before line 41. But the responseFormatter is a multi-line arrow function, so we need block-level disable. Let me fix the controller.

content.text: The tests expect `mockLoggerInstance.warn` to be called when execution time exceeds threshold. Let me look at the decorator implementation:
